info.title: Upflow API V1
info.description:
Upflow api documentation.

## Sandbox Environment

You can develop and test your integration in our API sandbox:
`https://api.sandbox.upflow.io/v1/`

## Reliability recommendations

Make sure to implement API call retries with exponential backoff for the
rare cases of API being down.

## Authentication

All requests must include the `X-Api-Key` and `X-Api-Secret` authentication
headers with each request. Project administrators can obtain these from the
settings menu.

## Addressing Models using `externalId`

All models contain an `externalId` field. You can use this field to store
your own identifier in Upflow.

If you opt for this type of integration, you can prefix ids with `external:`
in order to lookup Upflow models using your identifier.

Examples:

- `GET https://api.upflow.io/v1/customers/12345` returns the customer with
`id = 12345`

- `GET https://api.upflow.io/v1/customers/external:12345` returns the
customer with `externalId = 12345`

## Pagination

List endpoints are compatible with both page-based and offset-based pagination.

---
## Additional Information

- All amounts are stored as `Integer` and represent the lowest division of
the currency (e.g., cents for US Dollars).

- Currencies follow [ISO
4217](https://www.iso.org/iso-4217-currency-codes.html) (e.g., EUR, USD,
GBP).

- The API is rate limited to 600 requests per minute.

tags.name: Assigned Users
tags.description: Manage users assigned to customers (Account Managers).

tags.name: Customers
tags.description: Manage customers. Handles `external ids` on `customer` level.

post.description:
This endpoint can be used to create or update (if externalId already
exists) an invoice.

It cannot be used when using a native integration as invoices are only
created by the native integration in that case.

get.description: List invoices with optional filtering.

parameters.name: customer.externalId
parameters.description: Filter invoices by external Customer ID.

parameters.name: dunningPlanId
parameters.description: Filter invoices by dunning plan ID.

parameters.name: sortBy
parameters.description: Sort invoices by a specific column.

put.description: This endpoint can be used to update an invoice dunning plan, promise to pay or user defined custom fields.

get.description: This endpoint can be used to retrieve a specific invoice by its ID.

delete.description: This endpoint can be used to delete a specific invoice by its ID.

post.description: You can either send the PDF in a multipart/form-data request (i.e. a regular "file upload" in HTML) or a JSON request with the file encoded in base64.

This endpoint can be used to create or update (if externalId already
exists) a customer.

This endpoint cannot be used when using a native integration as
customers are only created by the native integration in that case.

contacts.description: Optional list of contacts to create alongside the customer.

get.description: List customers with optional filtering.

parameters.name: sortBy
parameters.description: Sort customers by a specific column.

parameters.name: balance.gte
parameters.description: Return customers having a balance greater than or equal to this value (in cents).

parameters.name: balance.lte
parameters.description: Return customers having a balance lower than or equal to this value (in cents).

parameters.name: amountDue.eq
parameters.description: Return customers where amountDue is equal to this value (in cents).

parameters.name: dunningPlanId
parameters.description: Filter customers by dunning plan ID.

put.description: This endpoint can be used to update an existing customer.

200.description: Customer Updated Successfully (Note: Apiary doc says 201, but PUT usually returns 200 on success)

get.description: This endpoint can be used to retrieve a specific customer by its ID.

contacts.description: List of contacts associated with the customer.

delete.description: This endpoint can be used to delete a specific customer by its ID.

post.description:
This endpoint is used to generate a portal URL for a customer. You can
link this URL in your app, or embed it in an iframe to allow your users
to access the customer portal from your app. To embed it in an iframe,
you must specify the `frameAncestors` attribute in the request.

The call to retrieve the portal URL must strictly be made from the
backend (not the frontend) as it utilises the client’s API secret.

requestBody.description: Options for generating the portal URL.

user.description: An email identifying the user who is using the generated URL and the token.

frameAncestors.description: Space-separated fully qualified domain names which are authorized to embed the portal in an iframe.

expiresIn.description: Expressed in seconds. Time span during which the token is valid. Default is 86400 (1 day).

embed_24h.summary: Embedding the customer portal, valid for 24 hours

url.description: The URL for the Customer Summary Page including the access token.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: Source system of the contact to update. Required if multiple contacts with the same ID exist for different sources.

total.description: Total number of assigned users for the customer.

total.description: Total number of assigned users for the customer.

It cannot be used when using a native integration as payments are only

parameters.name: amount

parameters.name: amount.gte

parameters.name: amount.lt

post.summary: Reconcile Invoices, Credit Notes, Payments, Refunds
post.description:
Manage a reconciliation, which assigns credit notes, payments and/or
refunds previously created to existing invoices.

A successful call will bind amounts and change invoices statuses
accordingly.

In order to avoid duplicating reconciliation, you have to provide a
unique `externalId` to identify it.
This endpoint can be used to create a new custom field.

get.description: List custom fields with optional pagination.

delete.description: This endpoint can be used to delete a specific custom field by its ID.

get.description: List actions with optional filtering.

parameters.name: sortBy
parameters.description: Sort actions by a specific column.

parameters.name: carryingInvoiceId
parameters.description: Filter actions by carrying invoice ID.

parameters.name: assignedToUserId
parameters.description: Filter actions by assigned user ID.

parameters.name: performedByUserId
parameters.description: Filter actions by the user who performed the action.

get.description: This endpoint can be used to retrieve a specific action by its ID.

examples: Can only update notes created via API

delete.description: This endpoint can be used to delete a specific note by its ID.

403.description: Forbidden - Cannot delete a note created from the UI

examples: Can only delete notes created via API

post.description:
This endpoint can be used to upload a file attachment to a note.
You can either send the file in a multipart/form-data request (i.e. a regular "file upload" in HTML)
or a JSON request with the file encoded in base64.

Limitations:
- Maximum file size: 5MB

requestBody.description: File to attach to the note.

file.description: The file to attach to the note.

403.description: Forbidden - Cannot add attachments to a note created from the UI

examples: Can only add attachments to notes created via API

delete.description: This endpoint can be used to delete an attachment from a note.

403.description: Forbidden - Cannot delete attachments from a note created from the UI

examples: Can only delete attachments from notes created via API

RangeQueryUpdateSpecs.title: Update range query params
RangeQueryUpdateSpecs.description: Only return entities that were updated during the given date range. (ISO 8601 format)

RangeQueryValidationSpecs.title: Validation range query params
RangeQueryValidationSpecs.description: Only return entities that were validated during the given date range. (ISO 8601 format)

RichTextDocument.description:
A document in Rich Text JSON format.
In the UI, it is rendered with [Slate.js](https://docs.slatejs.org/).

The document is a nested tree structure composed of the following node types:

**Element nodes (blocks)**

Block-level elements that structure the document content.
- `type` (string): The element type. Supported values: `"paragraph"`.
- `children` (array): An array of child nodes, which can be text nodes or inline element nodes.

**Text nodes (leaves)**

The lowest-level nodes containing the actual text content. Adjacent text segments with different formatting are represented as separate text nodes.
- `text` (string): The text content.
- `bold` (boolean, optional): Bold formatting.
- `italic` (boolean, optional): Italic formatting.
- `underline` (boolean, optional): Underline formatting.

**Link nodes (inline elements)**

Inline elements that wrap text nodes to create hyperlinks.
- `type` (string): Always `"link"`.
- `url` (string): The link URL.
- `children` (array): An array of text nodes representing the link text.

**Mention nodes (inline elements)**

Inline elements that reference a user.
- `type` (string): Always `"mention"`.
- `member` (object): The referenced member.
  - `id` (string, uuid): The member's unique identifier.
  - `displayName` (string): The display name of the member.
  - `email` (string): The email address of the member.

enabled.description: Change whether SEPA debit payments are enabled for this customer.

UserPosition.description: The role or position of the user within Upflow.

enabled.description: Change whether wire transfer payments are enabled for this customer. Setting to false will erase any bank account setting for this customer.

bankAccount.description: (Deprecated) Specify a singular bank account for the customer. If this field is assigned, it will remove all other enabled bank accounts and only keep the one indicated. Use `bankAccounts` instead.

bankAccounts.description: Specify the bank accounts that the customer can use. Leave `bankAccount` unassigned when using this field.

paginationOffset.name: offset
paginationOffset.description: The 0-based index of the first item to return.

paginationPage.name: page
paginationPage.description: The page number to retrieve (1-based). If both `page` and `offset` are provided, `offset` takes priority.

paginationLimit.name: limit
paginationLimit.description: Maximum number of items to return per page.

source.name: source
source.description: Data source for the contact. Recommended when using a contact ID that is external to Upflow.
